Title: A multifunctional sesquiterpene synthase integrates with cytochrome P450s to reinforce the terpenoid defense network in maize

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W22; B73 × M162W RILs; Goodman diversity panel
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-a2 clade II terpene synthase; sesquiterpene synthase with conserved DDxxD, NSE/DTE, and RxR motifs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: α-santalene/α-santalenoic acid branch and β-bisabolene-linked zealexin branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: α-santalene; β-bisabolene; additional minor sesquiterpene olefins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes (E,E)-FPP via a bisabolyl cation intermediate to generate multiple sesquiterpene olefins, primarily α-santalene and β-bisabolene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: α-santalenoic acid; zealexin D1; zealexin D2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate hydrocarbon precursors of target acids and contributes precursor flux to multiple zealexin branches
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pathogen-inducible accumulation of α-santalenoic acid and contributes to zealexin D1/D2 biosynthesis through production of α-santalene and β-bisabolene precursors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; linkage mapping; RNA-seq/proteomics induction profiling; heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with FPP; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with CYP71Z genes; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced in fungal-elicited stem tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in W22 stems after heat-killed Fusarium venenatum elicitation; protein also accumulates after elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat-killed Fusarium venenatum elicitation; fungal defense context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal defense against Fusarium graminearum and Fusarium verticillioides; broader maize immune defense
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key upstream scaffold-forming enzyme for α-santalenoic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Genetic mapping and mGWAS identified ZmTPS9 within the chromosome 10 TPS cluster as the major candidate underlying inducible α-santalenoic acid accumulation. 2. ZmTPS9-W22 is a multiproduct sesquiterpene synthase that converts FPP mainly into α-santalene and β-bisabolene. 3. These products are oxidized by CYP71Z P450s to α-santalenoic acid, ZD1, and ZD2, linking ZmTPS9 to multiple sesquiterpenoid defense branches. 4. A Thr413 deletion abolishes enzyme activity and explains nonfunctional alleles in α-santalenoic-acid-deficient lines.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: B73 allele is nonfunctional; deletion of Thr413 completely abolishes activity. The enzyme is catalytically promiscuous and may feed several zealexin branches.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering inducible terpenoid disease resistance; favorable functional alleles could be selected or edited, and stacked with CYP71Z genes to reinforce sesquiterpenoid defense output.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; proteomics; metabolomics; public datasets from prior W22 fungal-elicited tissues cited as Ding et al. 2019, 2020; accession/web links stated to be in tables but not provided in text

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W22 functional context; tested in Nicotiana benthamiana transient system
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CYP71Z family cytochrome P450, CYP71 clan
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oxidative tailoring of ZmTPS9-derived sesquiterpenes; also participates in diterpenoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ZmTPS9-derived sesquiterpene olefins including α-santalene and β-bisabolene
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: α-santalenoic acid; zealexin D1; zealexin D2; additional oxidized sesquiterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes ZmTPS9-derived sesquiterpene hydrocarbons to more oxygenated defense metabolites including acidic sesquiterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: α-santalenoic acid; zealexin D1; zealexin D2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms oxidized target products from terpene hydrocarbon precursors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of α-santalenoic acid, ZD1, and ZD2 from ZmTPS9 products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with ZmTPS9 followed by GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression profile similar to ZmTPS9 in fungal-elicited W22 stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in W22 stems following fungal elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat-killed Fusarium venenatum elicitation; transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream tailoring enzyme, but rate-limiting role not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCYP71Z16 showed expression coordinated with ZmTPS9 after fungal elicitation. 2. When co-expressed with ZmTPS9, it consumed sesquiterpene olefins and generated α-santalenoic acid, ZD1, and ZD2 plus unknown oxidized sesquiterpenoids. 3. It contributes to both sesquiterpenoid and diterpenoid defense metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported previously to function in both sesquiterpenoid and diterpenoid pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for stacking with ZmTPS9 to enhance oxidized terpenoid defense metabolites in crops.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression/proteomic context from previous W22 omics studies; no accession given in provided text

Co-expression with ZmTPS9 generated α-santalenoic acid, ZD1, and ZD2 and other oxidized sesquiterpenoids. 3. It contributes to both sesquiterpenoid and diterpenoid defense metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported previously to function in both sesquiterpenoid and diterpenoid pathways.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W22 functional context; tested in Nicotiana benthamiana transient system
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CYP71Z family cytochrome P450, CYP71 clan
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oxidative tailoring of ZmTPS9-derived sesquiterpenes; sesquiterpenoid-specific branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ZmTPS9-derived sesquiterpene olefins including α-santalene and β-bisabolene
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: α-santalenoic acid; zealexin D1; zealexin D2; additional oxidized sesquiterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes ZmTPS9-derived sesquiterpene hydrocarbons to acidic and other oxygenated defense metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: α-santalenoic acid; zealexin D1; zealexin D2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms oxidized target products from terpene hydrocarbon precursors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of α-santalenoic acid, ZD1, and ZD2 from ZmTPS9 products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with ZmTPS9 followed by GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression profile similar to ZmTPS9 in fungal-elicited W22 stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in W22 stems following fungal elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat-killed Fusarium venenatum elicitation; transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream tailoring enzyme, but rate-limiting role not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCYP71Z19 was co-expressed with ZmTPS9 upon fungal elicitation. 2. Co-expression with ZmTPS9 led to oxidation of sesquiterpene olefins into α-santalenoic acid, ZD1, ZD2, and unknown sesquiterpene acids. 3. Unlike ZmCYP71Z16/18, it is described as functioning exclusively in the sesquiterpenoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Sesquiterpenoid-specific among the three CYP71Z genes discussed.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful sesquiterpenoid-focused tailoring gene for engineering maize defense metabolites with reduced cross-pathway effects relative to broader CYP71Z enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression/proteomic context from previous W22 omics studies; no accession given in provided text
